rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks only into the implementation scope when binding to subcomponent ports or implementation-specific logic, otherwise use component type scope for visible feature contracts.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL structure by referencing only visible ports, types, and connections defined in the component scope.
Use implementation annexes for assignments tied to subcomponent outputs, not component type annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts in implementation scopes may use assign statements for output bindings; type scopes should use guarantees over visible features.
Equations and guarantees must use AGREE scalar types: int, real, bool — never AADL property names.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent paths or internal ports in component type annexes — they violate scope boundaries.
Do not embed AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) as variables in AGREE expressions.
